bioHotLightweight Curation — AI selection & significance rating
← bioHot

Biological Databases & Knowledge Graphs

New database releases, knowledge graph construction, ontology development, and FAIR data infrastructure.

28 papers curated · High: 2 · Medium: 22 · Low: 4 · Updated: 2026-07-10

Toxicology 2026-Jun-30 Medium

Crystalline silica particles exposure modulated miRNA expression profiles in human macrophage-like THP-1 cells.

This study investigates how exposure to crystalline silica particles alters microRNA (miRNA) expression in human macrophage-like cells, identifying potential biomarkers for silica-related diseases. The researchers exposed THP-1 derived macrophages to varying concentrations of silica and assessed cytotoxicity and miRNA profile changes. The findings suggest that silica exposure modulates specific miRNAs, which could serve as effect biomarkers for monitoring occupational and environmental exposure.

transcriptomicstoxicologyimmunology
Scientific data 2026-Jun-27 Medium

Molecular Safeguards of Survival: De novo transcriptome assembly and tissue-specific transcriptomic profiling of the yellow-foot clam Paphia malabarica.

This study presents the first high-quality de novo transcriptome assembly for the yellow-foot clam, a sustainably fished species in India, using Illumina sequencing. The assembly yielded nearly 56,000 predicted coding sequences, providing a foundational genomic resource for understanding the molecular biology and adaptive mechanisms of this economically important bivalve.

transcriptomicsgenomicstool
BMC genomics 2026-Jun-26 Medium

A single-cell transcriptomic atlas of the pigtail macaque placenta in late gestation.

This study presents a single-cell RNA sequencing atlas of the pigtail macaque placenta in late gestation, identifying diverse immune and non-immune cell types. By comparing these cells with human placental data, the work highlights similarities and differences at the maternal-fetal interface, providing a valuable resource for understanding pregnancy complications in a nonhuman primate model.

single-celltranscriptomicsimmunology
Trends in cancer 2026-Jun-25 Medium

Multi-omics analysis of extracellular vesicle cargo in cancer.

This review discusses how extracellular vesicles (EVs) from cancer patients carry diverse molecular cargo—including nucleic acids, proteins, lipids, and metabolites—that can be profiled using multi-omics technologies. The authors highlight that different molecular layers provide complementary insights into tumor biology, while also addressing methodological challenges in the field.

cancermulti-omicsreview
Nature communications 2026-Jun-24 Medium

MIND: multimodal integration with neighbourhood-aware distributions.

MIND is a new computational method that integrates multi-omics data (like genomics, transcriptomics, and epigenomics) without requiring imputation or sample exclusion, which can distort or lose information. It uses neighborhood-aware distributions to handle missing data and heterogeneity, improving cancer patient stratification. This approach offers a more robust way to combine different biological data types for better predictive performance.

machine-learningmethodologycancer
Journal of biomedical semantics 2026-Jun-23 Medium

A pragmatist approach to bridging tables and ontologies through LinkML and punning.

This paper presents a practical method using LinkML and punning to bridge the gap between flat tabular data and hierarchical ontology models, enabling richer semantic data integration. The approach simplifies the conversion of common tabular biodiversity datasets into ontology-compliant formats without requiring deep ontological expertise.

databasetoolmethodology
NPJ systems biology and applications 2026-Jun-22 Medium

Vitiligo Information Resource database v3.

VIRdb v3.0 is an updated integrative database for vitiligo research, combining curated transcriptomics data, protein-protein interaction networks, and libraries of natural compounds and FDA-approved drugs. It provides tools for pathway enrichment and molecular docking to help researchers understand the molecular basis of this autoimmune skin disease.

databasetranscriptomicssystems-biology
Medicine 2026-Jun-19 Low

Investigation of potential targets and mechanisms of naringenin in the treatment of spinal cord injury: A network pharmacology, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulation study.

This study uses computational methods (network pharmacology, molecular docking, and molecular dynamics simulations) to identify potential protein targets and mechanisms by which the natural compound naringenin might treat spinal cord injury. The findings suggest naringenin may act on multiple pathways involved in inflammation and cell survival, providing a theoretical basis for future experimental validation.

network-analysisdrug-discoverymethodology
Cell reports methods 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Spatial omics illuminates tumor heterogeneity.

This paper reviews how recent advances in spatial omics, single-cell technologies, and in vivo CRISPR tools are enabling unprecedented dissection of intratumoral heterogeneity (ITH), which is the coexistence of diverse cancer cell states and microenvironmental niches within a tumor. These technologies overcome limitations of conventional methods by resolving the spatial and functional complexity of tumor ecosystems, which is critical for understanding therapeutic resistance and disease progression.

spatial-omicssingle-cellcancer
STAR protocols 2026-Jun-19 Medium

Protocol for integrating and interpreting multi-omics data combining unsupervised and supervised data integrating approaches.

This protocol provides a step-by-step guide for integrating transcriptomics, proteomics, and metabolomics data using both unsupervised (MOFA) and supervised (DIABLO) methods, along with single-omics analysis and visualization techniques. It covers data preparation, model construction, and biological interpretation to help researchers gain insights from multi-omics datasets.

methodologytranscriptomicsproteomics
bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology 2026-Jun-17 High

MetaHarmonizer: robust biomedical metadata harmonization and a contamination control for inflated LLM performance on public benchmarks.

This paper introduces MetaHarmonizer, a tool for robust biomedical metadata harmonization that addresses key limitations of LLM-based approaches, including non-determinism, hallucinated ontology terms, and reliance on proprietary APIs. The authors also develop a contamination-controlled evaluation protocol showing that LLM performance on public benchmarks may be substantially inflated, questioning the true transferable capability of these models.

machine-learningLLMdatabase
The Journal of investigative dermatology 2026-Jun-16 Medium

Cutibacterium acnes induce T cell extracellular traps with distinct proteome programs.

This study shows that different strains of the skin bacterium Cutibacterium acnes can induce immune cells to release extracellular traps, with T cell traps showing strain-specific protein profiles that may explain differences in acne versus healthy skin.

immunologyproteomicsmicrobiology
Micromachines 2026-Jun-15 Medium

Next-Generation Bionic Sensors for Small Molecule Detection: Integrating Synthetic Biology, Nanomaterials, and Artificial Intelligence.

This review discusses how combining synthetic biology, nanomaterials, and artificial intelligence can improve bionic sensors for detecting small molecules, which are typically difficult to measure due to their small size and interference from complex samples. The paper covers recent advances in these integrated sensor technologies for environmental and biological applications.

synthetic-biologynanotechnologyreview
Trends in parasitology 2026-Jun-13 Medium

From parasite biology to predictive metabolic models.

This review discusses how genome-scale metabolic models (GEMs) are being used to better understand the complex metabolism of protist parasites, which cause major diseases worldwide. The authors examine key modeling decisions that influence how well these models can predict gene essentiality and host-parasite interactions, highlighting their potential for identifying new drug targets.

systems-biologymicrobiologyreview
BMC genomics 2026-Jun-12 Medium

Two Australian genome assemblies expand the genomic blueprint of giant kelp.

This study presents two new reference genomes for giant kelp from Australia, expanding the limited genomic resources available for this ecologically important species. These genomes will aid conservation and restoration efforts by enabling better population genomics analyses for remnant kelp forests threatened by ocean warming.

genomicsevolutionplants
BMC biology 2026-Jun-11 High

The first chromosome-scale genome assembly for Dermacentor reticulatus: a key vector of tick-borne pathogens of public and veterinary health importance in Europe.

Researchers have produced the first high-quality, chromosome-scale genome assembly for the ornate cow tick, Dermacentor reticulatus, a major vector of diseases like babesiosis and tick-borne encephalitis in Europe. Using long-read sequencing, this new genomic resource will enable deeper studies into tick biology, pathogen transmission, and the development of new control strategies. The assembly addresses a critical gap in genomic data for this expanding and medically important tick species.

genomicstoolevolution
Scientific reports 2026-Jun-11 Medium

Intelligent english education resource recommendation via LLM-knowledge graph integration and ecological niche fitness modeling.

This paper presents a framework that combines large language models with knowledge graphs and ecological niche fitness modeling to improve English education resource recommendations. The approach aims to overcome limitations of traditional systems by better capturing learners' linguistic competence, handling sparse data, and adapting to new learners and resources.

LLMmachine-learningtool
Clinical epigenetics 2026-Jun-10 Medium

CRONDEX: a web-based platform for exploring links between chromatin-related genes and neurodevelopmental disorders.

CRONDEX is a new web-based platform that systematically links chromatin-related genes to neurodevelopmental disorders (NDDs). It integrates phenotypic, functional, and pathway annotations to help researchers explore how mutations in chromatin regulators contribute to NDDs with overlapping clinical features.

databasetoolneuroscience
G3 (Bethesda, Md.) 2026-Jul-06 Medium

Multimodal genomic prediction is not a buzzword: why modern plant breeding must integrate genomics, enviromics, and phenomics.

This perspective argues that plant breeding has become a multimodal data ecosystem, yet most genomic prediction models still rely on a single data type. The authors advocate for integrating genomics, enviromics, and phenomics into unified prediction frameworks to fully leverage modern breeding data.

genomicsplantsmethodology
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-03 Medium

A knowledge graph and multi-agent reinforcement learning model for psychological risk identification and intervention in online learning.

This study developed a model combining knowledge graphs with multi-agent reinforcement learning to identify psychological risks in online learners and provide personalized interventions. Tested on data from seven courses, the model effectively detected declining motivation and task backlog, offering a new approach to supporting student mental health in digital learning environments.

machine-learningmethodology
Communications medicine 2026-Jul-03 Medium

Integrative proteogenomic analyses identify plasma proteins that impact the risk of ischemic stroke.

This study combined large-scale genetic data on plasma proteins and ischemic stroke to identify proteins that may causally influence stroke risk. By integrating proteomic and genomic analyses, the authors pinpointed specific plasma proteins as potential therapeutic targets or biomarkers for stroke prevention.

proteomicsgenomicsclinical
Bioinformatics (Oxford, England) 2026-Jul-02 Medium

MetagenomicKG: a knowledge graph for metagenomic applications.

The paper introduces MetagenomicKG, a knowledge graph that integrates multiple metagenomic reference databases (GTDB, KEGG, BV-BRC) to address nomenclature inconsistencies and facilitate exploration of microbial communities. This resource aims to streamline metagenomic analysis by providing a unified framework for querying genetic and functional annotations.

metagenomicsdatabasetool
Scientific reports 2026-Jul-01 Low

Artificial intelligence optimization model for public safety in smart cities based on multi-source data collaboration in the internet of things.

This paper presents an AI optimization model for public safety in smart cities that integrates IoT-driven real-time perception with intelligent decision-making, using Hefei as a case study. The model aims to overcome cross-departmental data barriers and improve governance efficiency through a 'technology-institution-data' collaborative framework.

machine-learningmethodology
Journal of food protection 2026-Jul-01 Low

Mechanistically Informed and Omics-Guided Essential Oil Applications for Food Preservation.

This review examines how essential oils can be used as natural food preservatives, focusing on their multi-target antimicrobial mechanisms and the challenges of compositional variability and instability. The authors propose that omics-guided approaches could help optimize essential oil applications by providing mechanistic insights into their activity.

microbiologyreview
Translational oncology 2026-Jul-01 Medium

Targeting mir130b-IL-33-PD-L1 axis effectively inhibits esophageal squamous carcinoma progression.

This study identifies a new molecular mechanism in esophageal squamous cell carcinoma (ESCC) where IL-33 signaling upregulates PD-L1 expression, promoting tumor progression. Using multi-omics and clinical data, the authors show that targeting the mir130b-IL-33-PD-L1 axis could be a potential therapeutic strategy for ESCC.

cancerimmunologytranscriptomics
PloS one 2026--01 Medium

Enhancing medical Q&A systems with multimodal knowledge graphs and dual-layer attention mechanisms.

This paper presents a new medical question-answering framework that combines multimodal knowledge graphs with a dual-layer attention mechanism to improve intent recognition, entity extraction, and cross-modal retrieval. The approach aims to overcome limitations in existing systems such as reduced accuracy in multi-label classification and heavy reliance on large-scale annotated data.

machine-learningclinicaltool
Frontiers in public health 2026--01 Low

Group cognitive space of online rumors in public health emergencies: a theoretical and empirical study.

This study explores how public cognition about online rumors during health emergencies evolves from initial formation to a stable 'group cognitive space,' which influences heterogeneous public behaviors. By analyzing the structure of this cognitive space, the authors aim to improve emergency governance and resource allocation during crises.

systems-biologyclinical
International journal of food science 2026--01 Medium

Microbial Ecology at the Nexus of Food Safety and Biotechnology With Ecological Mechanisms, Risks, and Emerging Innovations.

This review explores how microbial ecology connects food safety and biotechnology, highlighting the dual role of microorganisms as both contaminants and essential tools in food production. It synthesizes current knowledge on microbial interactions and environmental factors that influence food safety and biotechnological processes.

microbiomereviewmethodology
